tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting Minutes January 28, 2026 / Board Action Items February 2026

payload.title: MTA Board Action Items / MTA Board Meeting
payload.content_type: board meeting minutes and action items book
payload.description: MTA Board meeting minutes from January 28, 2026 and Board Action Items for the February 25, 2026 meeting, including committee reports, procurement actions, financial reports, and project updates.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Phase 2 of the Second Avenue Subway project extending north from 125th Street including tunneling from 120th Street to 125th Street, with new stations at 106th Street, 116th Street, and 125th Street
Formal kickoff of design and engineering for the Interborough Express project
Replacement of an 85-year-old facility with a modern depot capable of housing 272 buses, supporting full electrification and high-voltage infrastructure
Modernization of a major junction point operating 333 trains daily joining the Hempstead Branch to the Main Line; upcoming work requires service modifications for three weekends
CBTC signal modernization on the Fulton and Liberty Lines on the A and C in Brooklyn and Queens; covers 42.7 track miles and provides full underground cellular service through partnership with Boldyn
Sixth Avenue Line on the B, D, F, and M trains identified as the next CBTC project to advance

tool_args.label: Rockaway Line Resiliency and Viaduct Rehabilitation

payload.project_name: Rockaway Line Resiliency and Viaduct Rehabilitation
payload.description: Reconstruction of Hammels Wye, protective shielding, track rebuilding, and resiliency improvements to protect A Line service against extreme weather in the Rockaways; estimated completion cost $584 million
Completion of 39 subway elevator replacements and two railroad elevator replacements in 2025, eliminating the backlog of elevators beyond their useful life
Substantial completion of the OMNY fare payment system in 2025, achieving 98 percent adoption following the sunset of the MetroCard
Major upgrade to the Grand Central subway complex including new staircases, escalators, elevators, turnstiles, and circulation improvements, delivered with more than $46 million in savings

Accelerated reconstruction of the Grand Central Train Shed through public-private partnerships; Phase One completed $20 million under budget and $75 million in private contributions secured for subsequent phases
Completion of Phase One of the Park Avenue Viaduct ahead of schedule and under budget

tool_args.label: Elevated Structure Painting and Steel Repairs (Jamaica/Canarsie Lines)

payload.project_name: Elevated Structure Painting and Steel Repairs - Jamaica and Canarsie Lines
payload.description: Paint removal, abrasive blasting, repainting, and necessary steel repairs along the Jamaica Line between the Williamsburg Bridge and Delancey Street, as well as the Canarsie Line; estimated completion cost $217 million

payload.raw_value_text: almost two billion trips taken on New York City Transit, subway, bus and paratransit all together
payload.scope: New York City Transit subway, bus and paratransit

payload.raw_value_text: up 7% from 2024
Overall subway crime down 14% compared to 2019
now at 62%, which is a 13% increase over a year ago
Satisfaction with safety, is up 6 points, now to 63%
December was another strong month for Subways, with 82.8% weekday on-time performance
bus speeds continued to increase, up 1.2% year-over-year
95.4% of weekday trips meeting the thirty minute on-time performance threshold
968,000 trips in December, an increase of 2% from November
December ridership remained strong with 7.2 million riders, which is 94% of December 2019

tool_args.label: MTA Board Action Items Meeting February 25, 2026

payload.description: MTA Board meeting for Board Action Items including Safety, Capital Program, Finance, NYCT, LIRR, Metro-North, Bridges & Tunnels committees

tool_args.label: Congestion pricing first full year milestone

payload.description: First full year of congestion pricing celebrated by Governor Hochul and Mayor Adams; program has delivered less traffic, faster travel, safer streets, better air quality, and $15 billion for Capital Program
Winter Storm Fern impacted the region; MTA maintained operations as the only major transit agency on the Eastern Seaboard that kept operating

tool_args.label: New fare and ticketing changes effective January 4

payload.description: New fare and ticketing changes took effect on January 4 including extended fare validity, day pass, and free ticket redemption on LIRR and Metro-North
65th anniversary of the opening of the Throgs Neck Bridge

tool_args.label: MTA kept operating during winter storm

payload.claim_text: MTA was basically the only major transit agency on the Eastern Seaboard that kept operating
payload.subject: MTA
payload.description: MTA maintained operations during Winter Storm Fern when other transit agencies on the Eastern Seaboard could not

payload.claim_text: New fare gate prototypes are getting a lot of attention. There are three different models being tested across nine stations. That number of stations is going to double in the weeks to come. More than 2 million people have used them so far
payload.subject: MTA
payload.description: New fare gate prototypes being tested at nine stations, will double, with three models competing for the contract

tool_args.label: New fare gates in 150 stations in 2025-2029 Capital Program

payload.claim_text: Ultimately new faregates will be installed in 150 stations as part of our 2025-2029 Capital Program. The selected stations will account for over 70% of MTA ridership
payload.subject: MTA
payload.description: New fare gates planned for 150 stations covering 70% of ridership

payload.claim_text: MTA will take a serious look into the possibility of keeping the tunnel boring machine moving when it reaches the end of its path in this phase of the Second Avenue Subway. MTA will look to see if it may be continued west, perhaps all the way to Broadway to lay the core groundwork for the next phase of Second Avenue Subway. MTA is calling the Q West project, Q to the west – across 125th Street
payload.subject: MTA C&D
payload.description: Exploring keeping TBM moving west across 125th Street for future Second Avenue Subway phase

tool_args.label: Jamaica Station study with Port Authority

payload.claim_text: A study will be conducted jointly with the Port Authority regarding improving Jamaica Station. The station is the fourth largest train station, in terms of volume, in North America.
payload.subject: MTA
payload.description: Governor proposed creating a much better Jamaica Station; study will be conducted jointly with the Port Authority
